footer.tagline: A local-first fingerprint browser for multi-account, Web3, e-commerce, and automation teams. Profile data stays on your device or owned environment; Veil does not provide data hosting.

hero.eyebrow: v0.1.0 RC · Self-hostable · No data retention
hero.title: A local-first fingerprint browser for high-value multi-account teams
hero.subtitle: Create, run, and automate web account profiles from one workspace, combining isolated browser environments, stable fingerprints, dedicated proxies, and local encrypted data protection.
hero.description: Veil turns account environments into durable isolated assets with stable profiles, dedicated proxies, auditable automation, and local encryption.

features.title: Core Veil fingerprint browser features
features.description: Built around multi-account safety, stable fingerprints, automation access, and local data protection.

useCases.title: Stable profiles for outbound and automation workflows
useCases.description: Affiliate marketing, e-commerce storefronts, social operations, Web3 wallets, and scripted work all depend on reducing browser-environment correlation.

roadmap.title: From single-device complete to desktop source release

finalCta.title: Start verifiable multi-account management with Veil
finalCta.body: Use local-first storage, self-hosting, and auditable automation to keep critical account environments on your own devices and infrastructure.

gallery.title: Turn invisible security into pointable details
gallery.description: Three visual slices of the core mechanisms — fingerprint consistency, outbound connection audit, and hard isolation between profiles. Each one corresponds to a promise the code can verify.

tiers.title: Choose the plan that matches your team rhythm
tiers.description: All tiers include the same feature set: unlimited profiles, local encryption, self-hosting, and automation endpoints. Only duration and average monthly cost differ.
tiers.footnote: * Full refund within 7 days if not activated.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: If your question isn't covered below, please email support@useveil.xyz.

compare.title: How Veil differs from common fingerprint browsers
compare.description: We focus on independently verifiable differences: self-hosting, data retention, local encryption, and automation capability.
compare.footnote: * Pricing and feature sets may change. Confirm vendor websites before purchasing.

download.title: Download the Veil desktop app
download.description: Choose your system build. Activate it after purchasing a license; every release ships with SHA-256 checksums.

verification.title: How to verify your download
verification.body: Compute the hash with shasum -a 256 FILENAME and compare to the SHA-256 above to ensure the file hasn't been tampered with.

useCases.title: Veil is the shared environment layer for multi-account teams
useCases.description: Every workflow comes down to stable profiles, dedicated proxies, isolated sessions, and auditable automation.

automation.title: Profiles as callable browser resources
automation.description: Veil has one local management API: the HTTP API. After a profile starts, Veil returns a CDP WebSocket address that Playwright, Puppeteer, and Selenium use to control the page.

channels.title: One management API, one browser connection
channels.description: 7733 is the local Veil HTTP management API. The CDP WebSocket is the browser connection returned after a profile starts, not a second business API.

quickStart.title: Three steps from zero to a scriptable profile
quickStart.description: Generate an API key in the desktop app, start a profile over HTTP, connect via any CDP framework. The master password is never involved.

code.title: Pick your framework — drop in the snippet
code.description: Switch tabs to see the shortest working snippet per framework. All snippets are end-to-end runnable; copy and ship.

auth.title: API keys are fully decoupled from the master password
auth.body: Automation access is separated from human access by design. API keys live independently, scope-limited, and revocable on demand — they never touch the master password or recovery phrase.

changelog.title: Every release, recorded
changelog.description: What's new, what changed, what was fixed, known limits, and upgrade paths.

stack.title: Advanced browser data protection
stack.description: From master password to ciphertext on disk, every layer uses auditable standard algorithms. Compromising one layer does not grant direct access to the next.

trust.title: Security is not a slogan; it is an inspectable boundary

threats.title: What Veil protects against — and what it doesn't
threats.description: We honestly list both the threats we cover and the ones we don't. No security product is effective against attacks outside its threat model.
